actionUnits.condition: Validate component binding and defaults. MonoBehaviour_0 (id: 1486385207) on MainCamera is GvrPointerPhysicsRaycaster (script guid: eb5dd43baba2d4dc1bab789615567e3d) with raycasterEventMask.m_Bits=4294967295 and maxRaycastHits=64. No m_Calls (UnityEvents) present. Ensure scene has an active GvrPointerInputModule.Pointer and a valid PointerCamera so eventCamera getter returns non-null.

Raycast path (radius == 0) to drive Physics.RaycastNonAlloc in PerformRaycast(). Preconditions: GvrPointerInputModule.Pointer.IsAvailable == true; pointer.raycastMode set to Camera or Direct; PointerRay.distance long enough; pointer.CurrentPointerRadius == 0; At least 1 collider lies along the ray within finalEventMask. Expected: PerformRaycast() appends at least one RaycastResult; eventCamera, finalEventMask getters exercised; hit sorting and result append executed.

SphereCast path (radius > 0) to drive Physics.SphereCastNonAlloc in PerformRaycast(). Preconditions: GvrPointerInputModule.Pointer.IsAvailable == true; pointer.CurrentPointerRadius > 0; at least 1 collider within the sphere cast volume; finalEventMask permits collisions. Expected: SphereCast branch taken; results appended and sorted; screenPosition via eventCamera.WorldToScreenPoint evaluated.

No-hit early return path. Preconditions: Pointer available; pointer.CurrentPointerRadius set to 0 (Raycast) or >0 (SphereCast), but ensure no colliders on the path and/or eventMask excludes all relevant layers so Physics.*NonAlloc returns 0. Expected: PerformRaycast() returns false without appending results; finalEventMask path still evaluated.

Max capacity warning and dynamic resize path. Preconditions: pointer.CurrentPointerRadius set to 0 or >0; arrange >= 64 colliders along the ray so numHits == maxRaycastHits (64). Expected: Warning logged and MaxRaycastHits is doubled via setter, reallocating hits buffer at runtime (Application.isPlaying); ensures MaxRaycastHits property setter and internal buffer resize logic are covered.

Hybrid mode fallback path. Preconditions: Set pointer.raycastMode = Hybrid; ensure Direct ray produces no hits (e.g., no collider directly under reticle) but Camera ray does (collider along head-gaze ray). Expected: RaycastHybrid() first sets CurrentRaycastModeForHybrid=Direct, foundHit=false, then switches to Camera and PerformRaycast() succeeds; both branches covered.

actionUnits.condition: Autowalk MonoBehaviour (script_fileID=1625246635, guid=4b480839cc4455844a007a3d6fa53445) on GameObject 'Player' (fileID=1625246633) matches the provided script and scene settings: speed=2, walkWhenTriggered=false, walkWhenLookDown=true, thresholdAngle=45, freezeYPosition=false, yOffset=0. No UnityEvent m_Calls present. To trigger the 'look-down starts walking' branch, ensure Camera.main (expected child: Transform fileID=1486385204) eulerAngles.x transitions from <45 to a value >=45 and <=90. Expected: isWalking becomes true, Update() starts translating the Player forward based on camera forward (x/z) at speed 2.

With Autowalk still configured as walkWhenLookDown=true and walkWhenTriggered=false, simulate the camera pitching back up so Camera.main.transform.eulerAngles.x becomes <= thresholdAngle (<=45) OR >=90. This should hit the 'look-down mode stop' branch and set isWalking=false.

To cover the 'walkWhenTriggered only' toggle branches, Autowalk must be configured with walkWhenTriggered=true and walkWhenLookDown=false (different from current scene values). Simulate Input.GetButtonDown('Fire1') once to toggle isWalking=true, then again to toggle isWalking=false. Expected: movement toggles on/off without relying on head pitch.

To cover the 'combined look-down + trigger' branches, Autowalk must be configured with walkWhenTriggered=true and walkWhenLookDown=true (different from current scene values). Simulate Camera.main.transform.eulerAngles.x >= thresholdAngle (>=45 and <=90) and Input.GetButtonDown('Fire1') within the same frame to set isWalking=true. Then either press 'Fire1' again or rotate camera so eulerAngles.x >= 90 to stop walking.

To cover the y-freeze branch, Autowalk must be configured with freezeYPosition=true and yOffset set (e.g., yOffset=4.35). While isWalking=true (any branch), verify that Player.transform.position.y is forced to yOffset each frame.
